_comment: Static WER ceilings established via Harvard Sentence baseline benchmarks.
_vendor_note: `vendor` = each model's VENDOR/PAPER-PUBLISHED accuracy/WER. Each entry carries its own inline provenance: `source` (URL), `source_published` (where the figure originates), `verified` (how/when the number was confirmed against that source), and `accessed` date. All figures verified 2026-06-17. Corpus: Cloud/AssemblyAI + whisper-base.en + whisper-tiny.en are LibriSpeech test-clean; distil-small.en is LibriSpeech dev-clean (validation-clean) — the split its model card actually publishes (near-equivalent clean read speech, but a DIFFERENT split, so comparable-but-not-identical-corpus). Ranking on these clean read-speech sets: Cloud/AssemblyAI 97.29% > distil-small.en 96.57% (dev-clean) > whisper-base.en 95.8% > whisper-tiny.en 94.4%, matching real-world experience that Cloud is most accurate. They are NOT measured by SpeakSharp and NOT on harvard-list-1, so do NOT compare them to expectedAccuracy/floors above. IMPORTANT: LibriSpeech clean is easy, clean read speech; on diverse/real-world audio the small self-hosted Whisper models degrade FAR more than AssemblyAI (whose Universal Streaming mean across its published benchmark set is ~8.6% WER), so Cloud's real-world accuracy lead over Private is LARGER than the clean-corpus gap shown here.

_measurement_framing.policy: INTERNAL measurement context ONLY. Do NOT build a customer-facing vendor-vs-SpeakSharp accuracy comparison. The two sides are measured on different corpora/paths, so a head-to-head would falsely imply our small Whisper models beat vendor claims — they do not (our v4 base_q4 IS the vendor's whisper-base.en model and cannot exceed its own 95.8% published WER). Decision 2026-06-17 (option 2). An apples-to-apples LibriSpeech re-measure is a POST-LAUNCH backlog calibration task, NOT release-readiness work.

how_to_read.vendor: Published REFERENCE numbers on LibriSpeech (test-clean; distil-small.en is dev-clean) — vendor model-quality on a standard benchmark. NOT measured by SpeakSharp.
how_to_read.speaksharp_measured: Measured on Harvard Sentences (harvard-list-1), a DIFFERENT and easier corpus. Not comparable to the vendor LibriSpeech numbers.
how_to_read.node_clean_decode: Best-case model CEILING (full clean file, no streaming/mic). Not a real-use number.
how_to_read.browser_app_path: Harness-limited (Chrome fake-audio); NOT a quality gate. See _floors_note + /private/tmp/V4_BROWSER_PERF_DISPOSITION.md.
how_to_read.production_accuracy_winner: AssemblyAI (Cloud) is the more accurate production STT option. Private Whisper is valuable for privacy, cost control, local-first behavior, and fallback resilience — NOT superior accuracy.

vendor.source_published: AssemblyAI Streaming benchmarks, last updated Mar 2026
vendor.verified: WebFetch 2026-06-17: 'English benchmarks' row librispeech_test_clean = 2.71% WER (= 97.29% accuracy); page last updated Mar 2026.
vendor.realistic_note: 8.6% mean WER (~91.4% accuracy) across AssemblyAI's published Universal Streaming benchmark set (LibriSpeech clean/other, CommonVoice 11.81%, Earnings21 12.37%, TEDLIUM 7.81%, Meanwhile 6.73%, Rev16 12.99%), updated Mar 2026. This realistic figure is the better predictor of production accuracy; self-hosted Whisper base/tiny degrade much more than AssemblyAI off clean read speech.

Cloud.benchmarkPolicy: Cloud uses AssemblyAI Universal Streaming, not pre-recorded batch transcription. The release gate tracks the published/comparable streaming WER target (~8.14% WER = 91.86% accuracy); 95% remains a stretch target for a future batch/offline path.

history.note: Recalibrated from 95% batch-like target to AssemblyAI streaming/comparable published target. SpeakSharp Cloud app evidence on Harvard corpus: 8.47% WER / 91.53% accuracy.
Established baseline ceiling for Private STT

whisper-base.en.source_published: Whisper paper (Radford et al. 2022), Appendix Table D.1.1
whisper-base.en.verified: WebFetch 2026-06-17 (ar5iv HTML mirror): base.en LibriSpeech test-clean = 4.2% WER (= 95.8% accuracy). Prior 4.25%/'Open ASR Leaderboard' attribution corrected: the cited model card does not publish a test-clean WER; the Whisper paper does.

whisper-tiny.en.source_published: Whisper paper (Radford et al. 2022), Appendix Table D.1.1
whisper-tiny.en.verified: WebFetch 2026-06-17 (ar5iv HTML mirror): tiny.en LibriSpeech test-clean = 5.6% WER (= 94.4% accuracy). Prior 5.66%/'Open ASR Leaderboard' attribution corrected (see whisper-base.en).

cpu._floors_note: Legacy expectedAccuracy (93.89%) was measured on whisper-tiny.en (Node); the SHIPPING v2 Private default is whisper-base.en (see sttProviderConfig DEFAULT). The whisper-base.en|cpu floor (85.06%) is a BROWSER fake-audio measurement — HARNESS-LIMITED EVIDENCE, not a hard gate (see v4._floors_note + /private/tmp/V4_BROWSER_PERF_DISPOSITION.md). Authoritative model quality = the Node clean-decode ceiling.

history.note: Established baseline ceiling for Private (CPU) STT

base_q4.source_published: Whisper paper (Radford et al. 2022), Appendix Table D.1.1
base_q4.verified: WebFetch 2026-06-17 (ar5iv HTML mirror): base.en LibriSpeech test-clean = 4.2% WER (= 95.8% accuracy). Vendor reference = base model's published WER; the q4-quantized onnx-community port may differ slightly in practice.

distil_q4.verified: WebFetch 2026-06-17: model card eval reports 3.4326% WER (= 96.57% accuracy) on LibriSpeech validation/dev-clean. Corrected: prior entry claimed 3.48% on test-clean — the card reports dev-clean, not test-clean. Vendor reference = base distil-small.en's published WER; the q4-quantized port may differ slightly in practice.

v4._floors_note: DISPOSITION 2026-06-16: the `floors` below are BROWSER fake-audio numbers and are HARNESS-LIMITED EVIDENCE, NOT the release gate. Chrome --use-file-for-fake-audio-capture free-runs against a variable pre-record delay, so the SAME config varies widely (base_q4|webgpu measured 83.91% and a 15-word under-capture on back-to-back runs). Authoritative model-quality = the deterministic Node clean-decode ceiling (~99%; v4 base_q4/distil_q4 ≈ 98.85%). Real-world v2-vs-v4 = PostHog A/B post-Gate-B. Rollout treatment = base_q4 ONLY (distil_q4 stays flag-OFF, NOT a customer A/B arm). See /private/tmp/V4_BROWSER_PERF_DISPOSITION.md. The 2026-05-25 tiny.en entry is HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY.

history.note: HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY — early v4 worker mini-corpus on whisper-tiny.en (NOT a rollout model; v4 has no tiny variant). h1_1 11.11% WER, h1_2 25.00% WER, h1_3 0.00% WER. Retained as a data point; rollout floors use base_q4/distil_q4.

Native.benchmarkPolicy: Historical measurements are retained for comparison and future investigation. Native Browser STT is excluded from scheduled corpus/WER release benchmarking because Web Speech is browser/server/audio-route dependent.

history.note: Fixture/playback route produced junk transcript and should not be used as a Native release WER benchmark.

history.transcript: basically we should literally and that
history.note: Partial/unstable fixture-playback result retained for historical comparison only.

Chrome desktop real-mic journey produced visible transcript and save/history/detail/analytics proof with continuous=true, interimResults=true, maxAlternatives=1. No WER claimed because spoken ground truth was not captured as a corpus fixture.
